☍ Claude Hooks Contents Cheatsheet

A BookBank Field Guide · Claude Code

Claude Hooks

Turn a helpful agent into one that
obeys your rules — deterministically.

Told in the voice of The Crafter — calm, incremental, first-principles.
“A prompt is a suggestion. A hook is a law your tools can’t ignore.”
Grounded in the official reference at code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.

The Path

What we’ll build understanding of

Eight short chapters. We start with the mental model, learn the lifecycle and the data contract, then spend a whole chapter turning hooks loose on a real Elixir/Mix project — format-on-save, a compile guard, and a test gate that won’t let the agent stop on red.

  1. What a Hook IsThe mental model
  2. The Lifecycle MapEvery event, when it fires
  3. Wiring It Upsettings.json & handlers
  4. The Input ContractThe JSON on stdin
  5. Talking BackExit codes & JSON decisions
  6. MatchersTargeting the right call
  7. The Elixir WorkbenchRecipes that earn their keep
  8. Guardrails & GotchasSecurity, debugging, order
  9. CheatsheetEverything, one page
How to read this book Turn pages with the ← → arrows (or the buttons below); tap ↑ to jump back to Contents. Code is written for Claude Code’s hooks as documented in the official reference — copy it, adapt the paths, and run claude --debug to watch it fire.
Contents Begin ›
